findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the letter is missing in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the Great Seal of North Carolina centered at the top of the page. The HTML version does not display this seal.

findings.description: The DEQ logo in the footer of page 1 is missing in the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has a DEQ logo with department contact info at the bottom. HTML has the contact text but no logo image.

findings.description: The italic formatting of the document title in the body text is preserved in HTML, which is correct.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show the Regional Haze State Implementation Plan title in italics.

findings.description: The appendix list on page 2 loses the two-column tabular alignment (label: description) in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF shows appendix identifiers (e.g., 'Appendix B-3:') aligned in a left column with descriptions in a right column, creating a structured layout. HTML renders this as a flat bulleted list with colon-separated text, losing the columnar alignment.

findings.description: The handwritten signature of Michael A. Abraczinskas is missing in the HTML version on page 3.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a handwritten signature above the typed name. HTML only shows the typed name without any signature image.

findings.description: The HTML has no h1 or any heading elements at all. The document lacks any heading structure - no document title heading, no section headings. The entire content is rendered as paragraphs and divs without semantic heading hierarchy.
findings.evidence:
The HTML uses only

,

    ,
    , and
    tags with no h1, h2, h3, etc. headings anywhere in the document.

findings.description: The page 1 content appears to be duplicated - once as part of the embedded image alt text description area and then again as fully rendered HTML text. The entire letter content from page 1 is effectively presented twice in the HTML structure.
findings.evidence: The page header with ROY COOPER/Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER/Secretary, MICHAEL ABRACZINSKAS/Director and all the body text of page 1 appear both after the first <figure> tag and as the main body content. The HTML has nested <main> tags with the same content.

findings.description: The HTML has multiple nested <main> elements (at least 4), which is invalid HTML structure and creates a confusing document hierarchy.
findings.evidence: There is a top-level <main> wrapping the entire document, then additional <main> elements for each page's content, creating nested <main> tags.

findings.description: In the PDF, the reference to the CFR section reads '51.308(±)' (likely a scan artifact for '51.308(f)'), and the HTML correctly renders it as '51.308(f)'. This is an acceptable correction but noted for completeness.
The alt text for image 4 describes it as 'Blank scanned page 4' which is accurate since page 4 of the PDF is blank. Alt text for other images is generic but reasonably descriptive.
findings.evidence: alt='Blank scanned page 4' for the last figure; alt='Scanned letter from North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality, page 1' for the first figure.
